claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch implements a boundary audit system that detects project file mutations by full-tool Claude agents and blocks PRD readiness when violations occur. Provider adapter configures agent tool profiles and permission modes.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot() creates SHA256-based file snapshots and diffBoundarySnapshots() detects added/modified/deleted changes
evidence.why: Confirms explorer claim: boundary detection uses file hash digests to track mutations

evidence.summary: execution_policy sets project_mutation: forbidden and allowed_write_roots for artifact directory only
evidence.why: Confirms explorer claim: policy establishes contract that agents must not modify project files

evidence.summary: Runtime builds boundary snapshots before/after agent execution and reports violations in result.boundary with change list
evidence.why: Confirms explorer claim: boundary audit is integrated into dispatch runtime execution flow

evidence.summary: Boundary violations are mapped to BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers and override readiness.prd_ready to false
evidence.why: Confirms explorer claim: enforcement mechanism blocks PRD readiness on mutations

evidence.summary: Test proves boundary blocks status=blocked when agent writes unexpected-project-write.txt even with successful agent result
evidence.why: Confirms explorer claim: boundary enforcement works in practice and overrides agent success

evidence.summary: Test proves git-backed boundary detects untracked-mutation.txt as added change and triggers BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blocker
evidence.why: Confirms explorer claim: boundary system works with git repos and catches new file mutations

evidence.summary: evidenceScopeDeclarationIssues() filters evidence records lacking declared scope and generates EVIDENCE_SCOPE_REQUIRED blockers
evidence.why: Confirms explorer claim: evidence scope validation enforces explicit declarations before readiness

evidence.summary: buildProviderInvocation() sets Claude --permission-mode from config and handles claude_tools/allowed_tools/disallowed_tools arguments
evidence.why: Challenges explorer claim: File has 217 lines only; claimed lines 468-517 do not exist. Found permission-mode handling but no agent_tool_profile normalization

assumptions: Explorer may have cited incorrect line numbers for provider-adapter.ts executionConfig() or referenced an outdated version
Agent tool profile normalization to boundary/research/full may occur elsewhere in the codebase or be planned but not yet implemented
risks: If provider-adapter does not normalize agent_tool_profile, evidence agents may receive different tool access than expected
If permission-mode configuration is incomplete, full-tool agents might have unrestricted write access despite boundary policy
missing: Source location of agent_tool_profile normalization logic that maps to boundary/research/full profiles
Verification that claude_permission_mode:bypassPermissions is correctly set for full-tool evidence agents

result.notes: Core boundary audit claim confirmed: 7 of 8 evidence records verified with exact line matches. SHA256 snapshot detection, policy enforcement, test coverage, and scope validation all confirmed. One discrepancy: provider-adapter.ts has only 217 lines, not 468-517 as cited. Found permission-mode and tool filtering at lines 77-108 but no agent_tool_profile normalization. This is a documentation/line-number issue, not a substantive gap in the boundary system. The boundary audit mechanism works as claimed.
